input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10.

This is a known change in .NET MAUI. In .NET 9 (and earlier), Android used `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` to handle keyboard appearance, but this API was deprecated in Android 30 (API level 30). In .NET 10, MAUI moved to using the newer Android `WindowInsets` API and edge-to-edge display.
 
For .NET 10, the recommended approach is to use `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` to automatically adjust the view when the keyboard appears, or manually handle the insets if you need more control over the layout behavior. default on Android, which means I need to handle window insets properly. For .NET 10, the practical approach is setting `android:windowSoftInputMode="adjustResize"` in the manifest and using the built-in keyboard handling, or manually adjusting bottom padding when the keyboard appears. I can also use the platform-specific API to configure the soft input mode adjustment directly.

Since edge-to-edge is now the default, the key is ensuring the page content resizes correctly when the keyboard shows up by properly accounting for safe areas and insets through padding adjustments.
